message:
chore(beads): close frankenredis-zk9q (HGETALL iteration order)

 {"id":"frankenredis-ze9k","title":"redis parity: MONITOR should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks MONITOR as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/monitor.json, but monitor_cmd in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:7404 still returns OK without a script guard. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT handling and regression coverage for script calls.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:27.005302055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:45:34.210145223Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:45:34.208202014Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-zk9q","title":"[HIGH] HGETALL field iteration order differs from upstream (fixture captures upstream ordering)","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime case hgetall_result: upstream returns [name,alice,age,30] (insertion order), ours returns [age,30,name,alice]. HGETALL order is technically unspecified in Redis, but the fixture was captured from upstream. Either canonicalize in the harness (sort pairs by field) or switch our hash backend to insertion-order for parity with small-hash listpack encoding. Fix in fr-store Hash::entries iteration or conformance harness comparator.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:07:16.565993252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:07:16.565993252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-zk9q","title":"[HIGH] HGETALL field iteration order differs from upstream (fixture captures upstream ordering)","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime case hgetall_result: upstream returns [name,alice,age,30] (insertion order), ours returns [age,30,name,alice]. HGETALL order is technically unspecified in Redis, but the fixture was captured from upstream. Either canonicalize in the harness (sort pairs by field) or switch our hash backend to insertion-order for parity with small-hash listpack encoding. Fix in fr-store Hash::entries iteration or conformance harness comparator.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:07:16.565993252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:53:16.358964000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-24T06:53:16.358964000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in a236378: extended live_oracle_frames_match with HGETALL-specific pair canonicalization. HGETALL iteration order is explicitly unspecified in RESP; both impls are conformant, only the order differs. core_hash now 137/137 pass."}

 {"id":"frankenredis-zub","title":"Fix LTRIM and LRANGE index normalization bugs","description":"LTRIM and LRANGE incorrectly handle stop indices that are less than start or less than -len, returning or keeping the first element instead of an empty list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:15:20.278699584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.164920407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.162202704Z","close_reason":"Fixed LTRIM and LRANGE: clamp normalized indices before comparison to prevent negative-to-usize wrapping when indices are out of range","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
